conjugation_rule: 이 after consonant-ending stems; 가 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: subject marker (nominative particle)
examples: 책이 있어요. (There is a book.)
의자가 있어요. (There is a chair.)

conjugation_rule: 과 after consonant-ending stems; 와 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: used to mean and or with when joining nouns
examples: 밥과 국을 먹었어요. (I ate rice and soup.)
사과와 바나나를 샀어요. (I bought apples and bananas.)

meaning: used to mark the end point of time or place
examples: 서울에서 부산까지 기차로 가요. (I go from Seoul to Busan by train.)
아홉 시까지 기다렸어요. (I waited until 9 o'clock.)

meaning: subject marker (honorific, for respected persons)
examples: 선생님께서 교실에 들어오셨어요. (The teacher entered the classroom.)
할머니께서 밥을 드세요. (Grandmother is having a meal.)

conjugation_rule: 은 after consonant-ending stems; 는 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: used to contrast one thing with another
examples: 책은 읽었지만 숙제는 안 했어요. (I read the book but didn't do the homework.)
언니는 키가 크지만 저는 작아요. (My sister is tall but I am short.)

meaning: used to add the meaning of also, too, or even
examples: 저도 한국어를 공부해요. (I also study Korean.)
이 음식도 맛있어요. (This food is also delicious.)

conjugation_rule: 을 after consonant-ending stems; 를 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: object marker (accusative particle)
examples: 책을 읽어요. (I read a book.)
사과를 먹어요. (I eat an apple.)

conjugation_rule: 이랑 after consonant-ending stems; 랑 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: colloquial form used to mean and or with
examples: 국이랑 밥을 같이 먹어요. (I eat soup and rice together.)
친구랑 같이 영화를 봤어요. (I watched a movie with a friend.)

conjugation_rule: 으로 after consonant-ending stems (except ㄹ); 로 after vowel-ending stems or ㄹ-ending stems
meaning: used to show direction, means, method, or role
examples: 오른쪽으로 가세요. (Please go to the right.)
버스로 가요. (I go by bus.)

meaning: used to mark the starting point in time or place
examples: 아홉 시부터 일해요. (I work from 9 o'clock.)
어제부터 비가 와요. (It has been raining since yesterday.)

meaning: used to mark location, destination, or time
examples: 학교에 가요. (I go to school.)
서울에 살아요. (I live in Seoul.)

meaning: used to mark the person who receives an action
examples: 친구에게 편지를 썼어요. (I wrote a letter to a friend.)
선생님에게 질문했어요. (I asked the teacher a question.)

meaning: used to mark where an action happens or where something comes from
examples: 학교에서 공부해요. (I study at school.)
미국에서 왔어요. (I came from the United States.)

meaning: used to show possession or relationship, like of or 's
examples: 이것은 선생님의 책이에요. (This is the teacher's book.)
나의 꿈은 의사가 되는 거예요. (My dream is to become a doctor.)

meaning: colloquial form used to mean and or with
examples: 사과하고 배를 샀어요. (I bought apples and pears.)
나하고 같이 가요. (Go with me.)

meaning: only; just; exclusively
examples: 저만 한국어를 공부해요. (Only I study Korean.)
물만 마셔요. (I only drink water.)

meaning: copula (지정사); links subject to predicate noun or adjective
examples: 저는 학생이에요. (I am a student.)
이것은 책이에요. (This is a book.)

meaning: to; for (indirect object — colloquial, person)
examples: 친구한테 전화했어요. (I called a friend.)
언니한테 물어봤어요. (I asked my older sister.)

meaning: used when comparing one thing with another
examples: 오늘이 어제보다 더 추워요. (Today is colder than yesterday.)
언니보다 제가 더 커요. (I am taller than my older sister.)

meaning: expresses intention, supposition, or future
examples: 내일 일찍 일어나겠어요. (I will wake up early tomorrow.)
많이 배고프시겠어요. (You must be very hungry.)

meaning: used to mark an action or state in the past
examples: 어제 밥을 먹었어요. (I ate rice yesterday.)
어제 친구를 만났어요. (I met a friend yesterday.)

conjugation_rule: -으시- after consonant-ending stems; -시- after vowel-ending stems (honorific marker)
meaning: used to show respect toward the subject of the sentence
examples: 선생님께서 앉으세요. (The teacher sits down.)
어머니께서 오세요. (Mother comes.)

meaning: used to list actions or items
examples: 음악을 듣고 책을 읽어요. (I listen to music and read books.)
사과를 먹고 물을 마셔요. (I eat an apple and drink water.)

conjugation_rule: -으니까 after consonant-ending stems; -니까 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: used to give a reason, often when the speaker is making a judgment or suggestion
examples: 배가 고프니까 밥을 먹어요. (Since I'm hungry, I eat.)
시간이 없으니까 빨리 갑시다. (Since we have no time, let's go quickly.)

conjugation_rule: -으러 after consonant-ending stems; -러 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: in order to (purpose, used with movement verbs)
examples: 책을 사러 서점에 가요. (I go to the bookstore to buy books.)
밥을 먹으러 식당에 갔어요. (I went to the restaurant to eat.)

conjugation_rule: 아서 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여서 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해서); 어서 after all other stems
meaning: used to show a reason or a natural sequence of actions
examples: 배가 고파서 밥을 먹어요. (I'm hungry so I eat.)
비가 와서 우산을 가져왔어요. (It rained so I brought an umbrella.)

meaning: used to show contrast, like but or although
examples: 비가 오지만 나가요. (It rains but I go out.)
가격이 비싸지만 맛있어요. (It's expensive but delicious.)

meaning: used to express intention or purpose
examples: 건강해지려고 운동을 해요. (I exercise in order to get healthy.)
한국어를 배우려고 학원에 다녀요. (I attend a language school to learn Korean.)

conjugation_rule: 습니까 after consonant-ending stems; ㅂ니까 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: polite formal question ending (합쇼체)
examples: 밥을 먹습니까? (Are you eating?)
어디에 갑니까? (Where are you going?)

conjugation_rule: 습니다 after consonant-ending stems; ㅂ니다 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: polite formal declarative ending (합쇼체)
examples: 저는 학생입니다. (I am a student.)
밥을 먹습니다. (I eat rice.)

conjugation_rule: 읍시다 after consonant-ending stems; ㅂ시다 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: used to make a formal suggestion such as let's
examples: 같이 먹읍시다. (Let's eat together.)
빨리 갑시다. (Let's go quickly.)

meaning: used to make a polite request or polite question
examples: 여기 앉으세요. (Please sit here.)
이쪽으로 오세요. (Please come this way.)

conjugation_rule: -으십시오 after consonant-ending stems; -십시오 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: please do (formal honorific command)
examples: 자리에 앉으십시오. (Please take a seat.)
이쪽으로 오십시오. (Please come this way.)

meaning: adding a supplementary statement
examples: 저도 학생이에요. 언니도 학생이고요. (I'm a student too. And my sister is also a student.)
오늘 바빠요. 내일도 바쁘고요. (I'm busy today. And tomorrow too.)

meaning: used to make a suggestion or to wonder about something
examples: 우리 같이 밥 먹을까요? (Shall we eat together?)
지금 갈까요? (Shall we go now?)

conjugation_rule: 아2 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여2 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해2); 어2 after all other stems
meaning: informal polite statement or question (해요체 base)
examples: 오늘 뭐 해? (What are you doing today?)
밥 먹어? (Are you eating?)

meaning: is not (negation of the copula 이다)
examples: 이것은 제 가방이 아니에요. (This is not my bag.)
저는 의사가 아니에요. (I am not a doctor.)

meaning: used to express what someone wants to do
examples: 한국에 가고 싶어요. (I want to go to Korea.)
맛있는 음식을 먹고 싶어요. (I want to eat delicious food.)

meaning: used to show an action in progress or a continuing state
examples: 지금 공부하고 있어요. (I am studying now.)
친구가 음악을 듣고 있어요. (My friend is listening to music.)

conjugation_rule: 아야 되다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여야 되다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해야 되다); 어야 되다 after all other stems
meaning: used to express obligation or necessity
examples: 8시까지 와야 돼요. (You have to come by 8 o'clock.)
이 약을 먹어야 돼요. (You have to take this medicine.)

meaning: used to negate a verb or adjective
examples: 오늘은 학교에 가지 않아요. (I don't go to school today.)
이 음식은 맛있지 않아요. (This food is not delicious.)

related: -ㄹ 수 있다, <반의> -ㄹ 수 없다, -을 수 없다
meaning: used to express ability or possibility
examples: 한국어를 할 수 있어요. (I can speak Korean.)
피아노를 칠 수 있어요. (I can play the piano.)

meaning: cannot; be unable to (inability)
examples: 오늘은 밥을 먹지 못했어요. (I couldn't eat today.)
한국어를 잘 하지 못해요. (I can't speak Korean well.)

meaning: before (doing something)
examples: 밥을 먹기 전에 손을 씻어요. (I wash my hands before eating.)
자기 전에 이를 닦아요. (I brush my teeth before sleeping.)

related: -은 후, -ㄴ 후, <유의> -은 뒤에, -ㄴ 뒤에, -은 뒤, -ㄴ 뒤
meaning: after (doing something)
examples: 밥을 먹은 후에 산책해요. (I take a walk after eating.)
학교에 간 후에 공부해요. (I study after going to school.)

meaning: honorific form used to mark the person who receives an action
examples: 선생님께 선물을 드렸어요. (I gave a gift to the teacher.)
할머니께 전화를 드렸어요. (I called my grandmother.)

meaning: every; each (distributive particle)
examples: 날마다 운동해요. (I exercise every day.)
주마다 한 번씩 청소해요. (I clean once every week.)

meaning: only; nothing but (limiting particle, used with negation)
examples: 천 원밖에 없어요. (I only have 1,000 won.)
이것밖에 없어요. (There is only this.)

meaning: used to mean like or as when comparing things
examples: 얼음처럼 차가워요. (It's cold like ice.)
선생님처럼 잘 가르쳐요. (I teach well like the teacher.)

meaning: used to mean starting from a place or time
examples: 서울에서부터 걸어왔어요. (I walked all the way from Seoul.)
집에서부터 여기까지 걸어왔어요. (I walked all the way from home to here.)

meaning: onto; in addition to (additive location particle)
examples: 책상에다가 꽃을 놓았어요. (I put flowers on the desk.)
가방에다가 책을 넣었어요. (I put books in the bag.)

meaning: toward (combining direction + person indirect-object marker)
examples: 친구에게로 편지를 보냈어요. (I sent a letter to my friend.)
선생님에게로 다가갔어요. (I walked up to the teacher.)

meaning: from (source — person, formal)
examples: 친구에게서 편지를 받았어요. (I received a letter from a friend.)
선생님에게서 배웠어요. (I learned from the teacher.)

meaning: from (source — person, colloquial)
examples: 친구한테서 선물을 받았어요. (I received a gift from a friend.)
동생한테서 전화가 왔어요. (My younger sibling called me.)

conjugation_rule: 이나 after consonant-ending stems; 나 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: or; as many/much as (alternative choice or approximation)
examples: 국이나 찌개를 먹어요. (I eat soup or stew.)
커피나 차를 마셔요. (I drink coffee or tea.)

meaning: or (connecting two alternative actions or states)
examples: 집에 가거나 카페에 가요. (I either go home or go to a cafe.)
먹거나 마셔요. (I eat something or have a drink.)

meaning: contrast / opposition; background
examples: 학교에 가는데 비가 와요. (I'm going to school but it's raining.)
배가 고픈데 음식이 없어요. (I'm hungry but there's no food.)

conjugation_rule: -으면 after consonant-ending stems; -면 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: conditional; if; supposing that
examples: 시간이 있으면 와요. (If you have time, come.)
돈이 많으면 여행을 가겠어요. (If I had a lot of money, I would travel.)

conjugation_rule: -으면서 after consonant-ending stems; -면서 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: while; as (simultaneous action)
examples: 음악을 들으면서 공부해요. (I study while listening to music.)
웃으면서 이야기해요. (I talk while smiling.)

meaning: used to show purpose or an intended result
examples: 잘 보이게 크게 써 주세요. (Please write it big so it's easy to see.)
따뜻하게 입으세요. (Please dress warmly.)

meaning: interruption and transition (action stops midway and changes to another action)
examples: TV를 보다가 잠이 들었어요. (I fell asleep while watching TV.)
밥을 먹다가 전화가 왔어요. (A call came while I was eating.)

meaning: nominalization; turning a verb into a noun
examples: 수영하기가 좋아요. (I like swimming.)
한국어 공부하기가 어려워요. (Studying Korean is difficult.)

meaning: adnominal modifier (present tense)
examples: 지금 먹는 음식이 뭐예요? (What food are you eating now?)
한국어를 가르치는 선생님이에요. (He is a teacher who teaches Korean.)

conjugation_rule: -은 after consonant-ending stems; -ㄴ after vowel-ending stems (adnominal past/descriptive)
meaning: adnominal modifier (past tense)
examples: 어제 먹은 음식이 맛있었어요. (The food I ate yesterday was delicious.)
읽은 책을 반납했어요. (I returned the book I read.)

conjugation_rule: -을 after consonant-ending stems; -ㄹ after vowel-ending stems (adnominal future/presumptive)
meaning: adnominal modifier (future/prospective)
examples: 내일 먹을 음식을 준비했어요. (I prepared food to eat tomorrow.)
갈 곳이 있어요. (I have a place to go.)

meaning: adnominal modifier for past or completed state
examples: 닫힌 문을 열었어요. (I opened the closed door.)
배운 내용을 복습했어요. (I reviewed what I learned.)

conjugation_rule: -음 after consonant-ending stems; -ㅁ after vowel-ending stems (nominalizer)
meaning: nominalization; forming a noun from a verb
examples: 그의 웃음이 보기 좋았어요. (His smile was nice to see.)
성공함이 목표예요. (Succeeding is the goal.)

meaning: oh! (exclamation of discovery — informal)
examples: 오늘도 열심히 공부하는군요! (You are studying hard again today!)
그 사람이 벌써 오는군! (Oh, that person is already coming!)

meaning: I will (first-person promise or intention)
examples: 내가 먹을게요. (I will eat it.)
제가 먼저 갈게요. (I will go first.)

meaning: statement; question; command; request
examples: 빨리 와, 기다리고 있잖아. (Come quickly, we're waiting.)
그냥 말하지 그래요. (Just go ahead and say it.)

meaning: used to express surprise or admiration about something just noticed
examples: 벌써 다 먹었는데요! (You already finished it all!)
이 음식이 맛있는데요! (This food is really delicious!)

conjugation_rule: -네 is the plain/informal form; -네요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: used to express surprise or a newly noticed feeling
examples: 꽃이 정말 예쁘네요. (The flowers are really pretty.)
오늘 날씨가 춥네요. (The weather is cold today.)

conjugation_rule: -을래 is the plain/informal form; -을래요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: will you?; I want to (informal question or intention)
examples: 같이 갈래요? (Would you like to go together?)
뭐 먹을래요? (What do you want to eat?)

meaning: come to; end up; result in (unplanned change)
examples: 한국어를 배우게 됐어요. (I came to learn Korean.)
한국에 살게 됐어요. (I ended up living in Korea.)

meaning: because; due to (reason / cause)
examples: 피곤하기 때문에 쉬어요. (I rest because I'm tired.)
바쁘기 때문에 못 가요. (I can't go because I'm busy.)

meaning: decide to; agree to (decision / plan)
examples: 내일부터 운동하기로 했어요. (I decided to exercise starting tomorrow.)
같이 여행 가기로 했어요. (We decided to go on a trip together.)

related: -ㄴ 것 같다, -은 것, 같다, -ㄹ 것 같다, -을 것 같다
meaning: seems like; looks like (conjecture)
examples: 비가 올 것 같아요. (It seems like it will rain.)
그 사람이 피곤한 것 같아요. (That person seems tired.)

meaning: since; it has been (time elapsed since action)
examples: 한국에 온 지 3년이 됐어요. (It has been 3 years since I came to Korea.)
밥을 먹은 지 두 시간이 지났어요. (Two hours have passed since I ate.)

meaning: the act of doing; the fact that (nominalization)
examples: 운동하는 것이 건강에 좋아요. (Exercising is good for health.)
한국어를 공부하는 것이 재미있어요. (Studying Korean is fun.)

meaning: while; during (concurrent duration)
examples: 수업하는 동안에 조용히 하세요. (Please be quiet while class is in session.)
밥 먹는 동안에 TV를 봐요. (I watch TV while eating.)

related: -ㄴ 적이 있다, -는, 적이 있다 <반의>, -은 적이 없다, -ㄴ, 적이 없다, -는 적이, 없다
meaning: have (ever) done (experience)
examples: 제주도에 가 본 적이 있어요. (I have been to Jeju Island.)
한국 음식을 먹어 본 적이 있어요. (I have tried Korean food.)

meaning: used in notices or instructions to give a firm command
examples: 지시에 따를 것! (Follow the instructions!)
안전벨트를 착용할 것. (Wear a seatbelt.)

meaning: when; at the time of
examples: 어릴 때 공부를 열심히 했어요. (I studied hard when I was young.)
피곤할 때 쉬어야 해요. (You should rest when you're tired.)

meaning: thinking of doing; considering (intention) / afraid that; in case (concern about negative outcome)
examples: 비가 올까 봐 우산을 가져왔어요. (I brought an umbrella in case it rains.)
오늘 쉴까 봐요. (I'm thinking of resting today.)

conjugation_rule: 아보다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여보다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해보다); 어보다 after all other stems
meaning: try doing (attempt)
examples: 한번 먹어 봐요. (Try eating it once.)
이 신발 신어 봐도 돼요? (Can I try on these shoes?)

conjugation_rule: 아있다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여있다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해있다); 어있다 after all other stems
meaning: be in the state of (resultant state)
examples: 문이 열려 있어요. (The door is open.)
꽃이 피어 있어요. (The flowers are in bloom.)

conjugation_rule: 아주다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여주다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해주다); 어주다 after all other stems
meaning: do for (someone) (benefactive)
examples: 문을 열어 줬어요. (I opened the door for someone.)
가방을 들어 줬어요. (I carried the bag for someone.)

conjugation_rule: 아도 되다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여도 되다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해도 되다); 어도 되다 after all other stems
meaning: may; it is okay to (permission)
examples: 여기 앉아도 돼요. (You may sit here.)
이거 먹어도 돼요. (You may eat this.)

meaning: don't do (negative command / request)
examples: 걱정하지 마. (Don't worry.)
텔레비전 보지 말고 공부해요. (Don't watch TV; study instead.)
늦게까지 자지 마세요. (Please don't stay up late.)

meaning: have no choice but to (inevitability)
examples: 그 영화를 볼 수밖에 없었어요. (I had no choice but to watch that movie.)
기다릴 수밖에 없어요. (I have no choice but to wait.)

meaning: like; as; together (similarity / comitative)
examples: 선생님같이 말해요. (I speak like the teacher.)
친구같이 편하게 이야기해요. (I talk comfortably like a friend.)

conjugation_rule: 이고 after consonant-ending nouns; 고 after vowel-ending nouns
meaning: inclusive ('all' or 'every')
examples: 학생이고 선생님이고 모두 왔어요. (Both students and teachers came.)
일이고 공부고 다 잘해요. (He does well in both work and study.)

meaning: as; according to; right after (conformity / immediacy)
examples: 말한 대로 해요. (Do it as I said.)
약속한 대로 왔어요. (I came as I promised.)

meaning: from (source / origin — formal)
examples: 부모님으로부터 용돈을 받았어요. (I received allowance from my parents.)
회사로부터 연락이 왔어요. (A message came from the company.)

meaning: as much as; to the extent of (degree / proportion)
examples: 생각만큼 어렵지 않아요. (It's not as difficult as I thought.)
키가 저만큼 커요. (He is as tall as me.)

meaning: to (telling or complaining target — colloquial)
examples: 엄마보고 왜 그랬냐고 했어요. (I asked my mom why she did that.)
친구보고 빨리 오라고 했어요. (I told my friend to come quickly.)

meaning: only; just (exclusive limiting particle)
examples: 저뿐만 아니라 친구도 왔어요. (Not only I but my friend also came.)
이것뿐이에요. (This is all there is.)

conjugation_rule: 아 after consonant-ending names; 야 after vowel-ending names (vocative)
meaning: used when directly calling or addressing someone
examples: 영철아, 이리 와! (Yeongcheol, come here!)
민수야, 여기 와! (Minsu, come here!)

meaning: used to add politeness in casual speech
examples: 저도요. (Me too.)
맞아요. 그리고요? (That's right. And?)

meaning: used after a noun to quote what something is called or what someone says
examples: 집이라고 해요. (They call it a house.)
선생님이라고 불러요. (They call me teacher.)

meaning: past perfect; distant past (action completed before reference time)
examples: 그때는 학생이었었어요. (I had been a student back then.)
예전에 거기 살았었어요. (I used to live there before.)

meaning: used to mean if or when a certain condition is met
examples: 비가 오거든 전화해요. (Call me if it rains.)
시간이 있거든 와요. (Come if you have time.)

meaning: used to mention examples or possibilities in a list
examples: 먹는다거나 마신다거나 해요. (I do things like eat or drink.)
공부한다거나 운동한다거나 해요. (I do things like study or exercise.)

meaning: reason; because (quoting a reason or justification)
examples: 바쁘다고 못 간대요. (They say they can't go because they're busy.)
맛있다고 많이 먹었어요. (I ate a lot saying it was delicious.)

conjugation_rule: -으나 after consonant-ending stems; -나 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: used to present a contrast or opposition, often in a literary or old-fashioned tone
examples: 날씨가 좋으나 춥네요. (The weather is nice but cold.)
열심히 하나 결과가 없어요. (I work hard but there are no results.)

meaning: because of; while (cause = negative consequence from effort)
examples: 공부하느라고 잠을 못 잤어요. (I couldn't sleep because I was studying.)
숙제를 하느라고 늦었어요. (I was late because I was doing homework.)

meaning: so that; until; enough to (goal / degree / limit)
examples: 알아듣도록 천천히 말해요. (I speak slowly so that they can understand.)
빨리 오도록 전화했어요. (I called so that they would come quickly.)

conjugation_rule: 아다가 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여다가 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해다가); 어다가 after all other stems
meaning: brings and does; fetches and then does (transfer + action)
examples: 마트에서 사다가 냉장고에 넣었어요. (I bought it from the market and put it in the fridge.)
도서관에서 빌려다가 읽었어요. (I borrowed it from the library and read it.)

conjugation_rule: 아도 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여도 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해도); 어도 after all other stems
meaning: even if; even though (concession)
examples: 비가 와도 가요. (Even if it rains, I go.)
돈이 없어도 행복해요. (Even without money, I am happy.)

conjugation_rule: 아야 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여야 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해야); 어야 after all other stems
meaning: only if; must (strong necessary condition)
examples: 열심히 공부해야 해요. (You have to study hard.)
밥을 먹어야 힘이 나요. (You have to eat to have energy.)

conjugation_rule: 아야지1 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여야지1 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해야지1); 어야지1 after all other stems
meaning: necessary condition
examples: 일찍 자야지. (I should sleep early.)
빨리 가야지. (I should go quickly.)

meaning: and then found; because in the past (past retrospective)
examples: 약을 먹었더니 나았어요. (I took medicine and got better.)
음식을 먹었더니 배가 불러요. (I ate food and now I'm full.)

meaning: as soon as (immediacy)
examples: 집에 도착하자마자 전화했어요. (I called as soon as I arrived home.)
밥을 먹자마자 나갔어요. (I went out as soon as I finished eating.)

meaning: continuation leading to negative consequence (warning against continuing)
examples: 그렇게 놀다가 시험에 떨어져요. (If you keep playing like that, you will fail.)
늦게 자다가 지각해요. (If you keep sleeping late, you will be late.)

meaning: used to give a reason or cause for what follows
examples: 날씨가 좋으니 산책하러 가요. (The weather is nice so let's go for a walk.)
피곤하니 쉬세요. (You're tired so please rest.)

conjugation_rule: -으려면 after consonant-ending stems; -려면 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: if one intends to; in order to (conditional intention)
examples: 책을 읽으려면 시간이 필요해요. (If you want to read a book, you need time.)
한국어를 잘 하려면 매일 연습해야 해요. (If you want to speak Korean well, you have to practice every day.)

meaning: adnominal modifier (retrospective; past habit or incomplete action)
examples: 예전에 살던 집이 그리워요. (I miss the house I used to live in.)
자주 가던 카페가 문을 닫았어요. (The cafe I used to frequent has closed.)

meaning: used at the end of a sentence to give a reason or explanation
examples: 제가 바쁘거든요. 나중에 할게요. (I'm busy, you see. I'll do it later.)
그 사람이 착하거든요. 그래서 좋아해요. (You see, he's kind. That's why I like him.)

meaning: oh! (exclamation of fresh discovery — informal)
examples: 비가 이렇게 많이 오는구나! (Oh, it is raining this much!)
그 친구가 벌써 졸업하는구나! (Oh, that friend is already graduating!)

meaning: plain style declarative (neutral / literary declarative)
examples: 매일 운동을 한다. (I exercise every day.)
학생들이 열심히 공부한다. (Students study hard.)

meaning: used to express surprise or admiration based on what one has noticed or experienced
examples: 그 책이 재미있던데요! (That book was really interesting!)
생각보다 맛있던데요! (It was more delicious than I thought!)

conjugation_rule: -잖아 is the plain/informal form; -잖아요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: you know; isn't it (seeking agreement — informal)
examples: 우리 약속이 있잖아요. (We have an appointment, you know.)
그 사람 착하잖아요. (That person is kind, isn't he?)

meaning: suggestion / let's
examples: 우리 같이 먹자. (Let's eat together.)
빨리 가자. (Let's go quickly.)

meaning: used to ask a question, often in a familiar tone
examples: 벌써 다 먹었니? (Did you already eat everything?)
숙제 다 했니? (Did you finish your homework?)

meaning: make; let; have (someone do something) (causative)
examples: 아이를 웃게 했어요. (I made the child smile.)
학생들이 열심히 공부하게 해요. (I make the students study hard.)

meaning: after doing (completion, then moves to next action)
examples: 밥을 먹고 나서 설거지를 해요. (I do the dishes after eating.)
숙제를 끝내고 나서 놀아요. (I play after finishing homework.)

meaning: end up doing; finally (regret or inevitability)
examples: 비밀을 말하고 말았어요. (I ended up telling the secret.)
늦게 일어나고 말았어요. (I ended up waking up late.)

meaning: want to (third-person desire expression)
examples: 동생이 아이스크림을 먹고 싶어 해요. (My younger sibling wants to eat ice cream.)
친구가 한국에 가고 싶어 해요. (My friend wants to go to Korea.)

meaning: as a result of (result / outcome)
examples: 열심히 공부한 결과 시험에 합격했어요. (As a result of studying hard, I passed the exam.)
꾸준히 노력한 결과 성공했어요. (As a result of steady effort, I succeeded.)

meaning: after (doing something)
examples: 밥을 먹은 다음에 산책해요. (I take a walk after eating.)
숙제를 한 다음에 놀아요. (I play after doing homework.)

meaning: instead of; in exchange for
examples: 밥을 먹는 대신에 과일을 먹었어요. (Instead of eating rice, I ate fruit.)
공부하는 대신에 운동을 했어요. (Instead of studying, I exercised.)

meaning: as much as; proportionally; because (degree / reason)
examples: 열심히 하는 만큼 결과가 좋아요. (The results are good as much as you work hard.)
노력하는 만큼 실력이 늘어요. (Your skills improve as much as you practice.)

meaning: on the other hand; whereas (contrast)
examples: 오빠는 외향적인 반면 저는 내성적이에요. (My brother is extroverted whereas I am introverted.)
영어는 쉬운 반면 한국어는 어려워요. (English is easy whereas Korean is difficult.)

meaning: seems that; I guess (conjecture from evidence)
examples: 오늘 피곤한가 봐요. (You seem tired today.)
밖에 비가 오나 봐요. (It seems like it's raining outside.)

meaning: I expect it would be; I think so but (supposition with concern)
examples: 지금쯤 도착했을 텐데 왜 연락이 없죠? (They should have arrived by now, but why no contact?)
배가 고플 텐데 뭐 먹을래요? (You must be hungry; would you like something to eat?)

related: -기 위해서, -기 위한, 을 위해, 를 위해
conjugation_rule: 하여 form contracts to 해 in colloquial speech
meaning: in order to; for the purpose of
examples: 건강을 위해 운동해요. (I exercise for my health.)
시험에 합격하기 위해 공부해요. (I study in order to pass the exam.)

meaning: if it weren't for; as long as it's not
examples: 너만 아니면 다 괜찮아. (Everything is fine as long as it's not you.)
이 문제만 아니면 괜찮아요. (It would be fine if it weren't for this problem.)

meaning: must not; should not (prohibition)
examples: 여기서 사진 찍으면 안 돼요. (You must not take pictures here.)
수업 중에 전화하면 안 돼요. (You must not make calls during class.)

meaning: I wish; I hope (wish / desire)
examples: 내일 비가 오지 않으면 좋겠어요. (I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow.)
빨리 나으면 좋겠어요. (I hope you recover quickly.)

conjugation_rule: 아가다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여가다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해가다); 어가다 after all other stems
meaning: do gradually; go doing (progressive movement away from speaker)
examples: 한국어 실력이 점점 늘어 가요. (My Korean skills are gradually improving.)
날이 밝아 가요. (The day is gradually getting brighter.)

conjugation_rule: 아가지고 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여가지고 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해가지고); 어가지고 after all other stems
meaning: by doing; having done (colloquial sequential reason)
examples: 음식을 사 가지고 집에 왔어요. (I bought food and brought it home.)
물을 사 가지고 왔어요. (I bought water and brought it.)

conjugation_rule: 아놓다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여놓다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해놓다); 어놓다 after all other stems
meaning: do in advance and leave (completed preparatory action)
examples: 음식을 만들어 놓았어요. (I made food in advance.)
메모를 적어 놓았어요. (I wrote down a memo in advance.)

conjugation_rule: 아두다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여두다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해두다); 어두다 after all other stems
meaning: keep in a state; do for future use (preparatory state)
examples: 나중을 위해 돈을 아껴 두었어요. (I saved money for later.)
음식을 냉장고에 넣어 두었어요. (I kept the food in the refrigerator.)

conjugation_rule: 아드리다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여드리다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해드리다); 어드리다 after all other stems
meaning: do for someone (honorific benefactive)
examples: 짐을 들어 드릴게요. (I will carry your luggage for you.)
할머니께 문을 열어 드렸어요. (I opened the door for my grandmother.)

conjugation_rule: 아야겠- when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여야겠- after 하다 stems (contracted to 해야겠-); 어야겠- after all other stems
meaning: should; I really must (strong personal resolution)
examples: 운동을 더 열심히 해야겠어요. (I really must exercise harder.)
일찍 자야겠어요. (I really must sleep early.)

conjugation_rule: 아지다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여지다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해지다); 어지다 after all other stems
meaning: become; get (passive change of state)
examples: 날씨가 따뜻해졌어요. (The weather has gotten warmer.)
방이 밝아졌어요. (The room has gotten brighter.)

related: 에 대해, 에 대해서, 에 대한
conjugation_rule: 하여 form contracts to 해 in colloquial speech
meaning: about; regarding; concerning
examples: 환경에 대하여 이야기했어요. (We talked about the environment.)
그 사건에 대하여 설명해 주세요. (Please explain about that incident.)

meaning: I expect / plan to, so (supposition + consequence)
examples: 내가 할 테니 기다려요. (I'll do it, so wait.)
비가 올 테니 우산을 가져가요. (It will rain, so take an umbrella.)

conjugation_rule: 아오다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여오다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해오다); 어오다 after all other stems
meaning: have been doing; come doing (progressive toward speaker)
examples: 10년 전부터 이 일을 해 왔어요. (I have been doing this work for 10 years.)
꾸준히 노력해 왔어요. (I have been making steady efforts.)

meaning: indeed; though I grant that (concessive acknowledgment)
examples: 가기는 가는데 별로 안 좋아요. (I do go, but I don't really like it.)
먹기는 먹었는데 배가 안 불러요. (I did eat, but I'm not full.)

meaning: seems like; appears to (inference from observation)
examples: 아직 안 온 걸 보니 바쁜 모양이에요. (Since he hasn't come yet, he seems busy.)
그 사람이 화가 난 모양이에요. (That person seems to be angry.)

meaning: tends to be; rather; on the side of (general tendency)
examples: 저는 매운 걸 잘 먹는 편이에요. (I tend to eat spicy food well.)
저는 조용한 편이에요. (I tend to be quiet.)

meaning: seems that (conjecture — slightly surprised)
examples: 저 사람이 사장인가 봐요. (That person seems to be the manager.)
그 학생이 공부를 잘하는가 봐요. (That student seems to be good at studying.)

meaning: in the middle of; currently doing (progressive state)
examples: 지금 밥을 먹는 중이에요. (I am in the middle of eating.)
회의를 하는 중이에요. (We are in the middle of a meeting.)

meaning: was about to but (interrupted intention)
examples: 나가려다가 비가 와서 못 나갔어요. (I was about to go out but couldn't because it rained.)
전화하려다가 바빠서 못 했어요. (I was about to call but was too busy.)

conjugation_rule: 아보이다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여보이다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해보이다); 어보이다 after all other stems
meaning: looks; appears; seems (visual impression)
examples: 피곤해 보여요. (You look tired.)
행복해 보여요. (You look happy.)

meaning: indirect quotation; reporting what someone said or commanded
examples: 그가 결혼한다고 했어요. (He said he was getting married.)
선생님이 숙제를 하라고 했어요. (The teacher said to do the homework.)

meaning: let alone; far from (emphasizes impossibility of even lesser thing)
examples: 밥은커녕 물도 못 마셨어요. (I couldn't even drink water, let alone eat rice.)
친구는커녕 가족도 없어요. (There's no family, let alone friends.)

meaning: even though it is only; at least (make do with less)
examples: 이것이나마 드세요. (Please at least have this.)
돈이 없지만 이것이나마 드릴게요. (I have no money, but let me at least give you this.)

conjugation_rule: 이며 after consonant-ending nouns; 며 after vowel-ending nouns
meaning: used to list multiple things, often in a formal or written style
examples: 책이며 공책이며 다 샀어요. (I bought books, notebooks, and everything.)
사과며 배며 과일을 많이 먹어요. (I eat lots of fruit including apples and pears.)

meaning: choice; whether ... or; no matter which
examples: 남자이든 여자이든 다 환영해요. (Whether man or woman, everyone is welcome.)
커피이든 차이든 좋아요. (Whether coffee or tea, it is fine.)

conjugation_rule: 이란 after consonant-ending nouns; 란 after vowel-ending nouns
meaning: definition
examples: 사랑이란 상대를 배려하는 것이에요. (Love is caring for the other person.)
자유란 소중한 거예요. (Freedom is precious.)

conjugation_rule: 이면 after consonant-ending nouns; 면 after vowel-ending nouns
meaning: designation
examples: 이것이면 충분해요. (If it's this, it's enough.)
바다면 좋겠어요. (It would be nice if it were the sea.)

conjugation_rule: 이야 after consonant-ending nouns; 야 after vowel-ending nouns
meaning: used to strongly emphasize the topic or point being made
examples: 이 일이야말로 제가 하고 싶은 일이에요. (This work is truly what I want to do.)
지금이야말로 시작할 때예요. (Now is truly the time to start.)

meaning: for; considering; as (comparison within category, often concessive)
examples: 학생치고 공부 안 하는 사람이 있나요? (Is there a student who doesn't study?)
이 나이치고 젊어 보여요. (For this age, they look young.)

meaning: even; as well (exhaustive addition, similar to 마저)
examples: 친구까지 떠나 버렸어요. (Even my friend left.)
마지막 희망까지 사라졌어요. (Even the last hope has disappeared.)

conjugation_rule: 이라도 after consonant-ending nouns; 라도 after vowel-ending nouns
meaning: second-best option; at least; settling for less
examples: 잠이라도 자야겠어요. (I should at least sleep.)
커피라도 마셔요. (At least drink some coffee.)

conjugation_rule: 으로서 after consonant-ending stems (except ㄹ); 로서 otherwise
meaning: as (in one's role or capacity as)
examples: 학생으로서 열심히 공부해야 해요. (As a student, I must study hard.)
의사로서 환자를 돌봐야 해요. (As a doctor, I must take care of patients.)

conjugation_rule: 으로써 after consonant-ending stems (except ㄹ); 로써 otherwise
meaning: by means of; with (instrument / means)
examples: 노력으로써 성공했어요. (I succeeded through effort.)
대화로써 문제를 해결했어요. (I solved the problem through dialogue.)

meaning: even; too; as well (final addition, often negative)
examples: 친구마저 나를 모른 척했어요. (Even my friend ignored me.)
돈마저 없어졌어요. (Even the money is gone.)

meaning: of course; and also (acknowledgment + addition — literary)
examples: 그 사람은 착하거니와 능력도 있어요. (He is kind and also capable.)
경치가 아름답거니와 공기도 맑아요. (The scenery is beautiful, and the air is also clean.)

meaning: even after doing; despite having done (contrast after action)
examples: 거짓말을 하고도 사과를 안 해요. (Even after lying, they don't apologize.)
밥을 먹고도 배가 고파요. (Even after eating, I'm still hungry.)

meaning: in order to; wanting to (formal intention / purpose)
examples: 환경을 보호하고자 노력해요. (I make efforts to protect the environment.)
더 나은 삶을 살고자 일을 해요. (I work in order to live a better life.)

meaning: because; since; to (reason prompting speaker's reaction)
examples: 날씨가 좋기에 산책을 나갔어요. (The weather was nice so I went for a walk.)
늦었기에 서둘렀어요. (I hurried because I was late.)

meaning: used to refer to an unknown question or to express wonder about something
examples: 어디 사는지 몰라요. (I don't know where they live.)
뭘 먹는지 궁금해요. (I'm curious about what they're eating.)

meaning: as you know; just as (shared knowledge reference)
examples: 아시다시피 그 문제는 복잡해요. (As you know, that problem is complicated.)
말씀하셨다시피 노력이 필요해요. (As you said, effort is needed.)

meaning: even if (strong concession — regardless of condition)
examples: 비가 오더라도 갈 거예요. (Even if it rains, I will go.)
힘들더라도 포기하지 마세요. (Even if it's difficult, don't give up.)

meaning: choice; either ... or; whichever
examples: 커피든지 차든지 마셔요. (Drink coffee or tea, whichever you like.)
가든지 오든지 연락해요. (Whether you go or come, contact me.)

conjugation_rule: -으므로 after consonant-ending stems; -므로 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: because; since (formal written reason / cause)
examples: 시간이 없으므로 서두릅시다. (Since there is no time, let's hurry.)
비가 오므로 외출을 안 했어요. (Since it rained, I didn't go out.)

conjugation_rule: 을래야 after consonant-ending stems; ㄹ래야 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: no matter how much one tries (impossibility despite effort)
examples: 먹을래야 입맛이 없어요. (I can't eat no matter how much I try.)
잘래야 잠이 안 와요. (I cannot sleep no matter how much I try.)

meaning: only after doing; having done as prerequisite (sequential)
examples: 다 끝내고서 쉬었어요. (I rested only after finishing everything.)
밥을 먹고서 나갔어요. (I went out only after eating.)

meaning: hypothetical condition for a verb action; if (someone does or says)
examples: 내일 온다면 같이 가요. (If they say they are coming tomorrow, let us go together.)
열심히 한다면 꼭 성공할 거예요. (If one works hard, they will surely succeed.)

meaning: and then; but (change from past state or action)
examples: 어제는 맑더니 오늘은 비가 와요. (Yesterday it was clear but today it's raining.)
밥을 잘 먹더니 갑자기 나갔어요. (That person was eating well but suddenly left.)

meaning: used to show contrast or opposition, often in a literary or old-fashioned tone
examples: 비가 오던데 우산 가져왔어요? (It was raining; did you bring an umbrella?)
음식이 맛있던데 또 오고 싶어요. (The food was delicious; I want to come again.)

meaning: as if; just as (similarity / analogy)
examples: 새처럼 날고 싶어요. (I want to fly like a bird.)
알듯이 모르는 것도 있어요. (There are things I half-know as well.)

conjugation_rule: 을수록 after consonant-ending stems; ㄹ수록 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: the more ... the more (proportional increase)
examples: 공부할수록 모르는 게 많아요. (The more I study, the more I don't know.)
갈수록 어려워져요. (It gets harder as time goes on.)

conjugation_rule: -으며 after consonant-ending stems; -며 after vowel-ending stems
meaning: used to connect actions or statements in a formal or written style
examples: 노래하며 춤을 춰요. (I sing and dance.)
웃으며 인사했어요. (I greeted while smiling.)

meaning: used to react with surprise or emotion to something one has heard
examples: 그 사람이 온다니 정말 반가워요. (The news that that person is coming makes me so glad.)
이제 끝난다니 믿기 어려워요. (It is hard to believe that it is finally over.)

conjugation_rule: -더군 is the plain/informal form; -더군요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: turns out; I saw that (retrospective personal discovery)
examples: 그 영화가 재미있더군요! (That movie was really interesting, it turns out!)
날씨가 좋더군. (The weather was good, I see.)

meaning: I found that; I recall that (retrospective statement — first person)
examples: 어제 거기 갔더라. (I went there yesterday, I recall.)
그 음식이 생각보다 맛있더라. (That food was more delicious than I thought.)

conjugation_rule: 아라1 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여라1 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해라1); 어라1 after all other stems
meaning: used to give a direct command
examples: 빨리 먹어라. (Eat quickly!)
이리 와라. (Come here!)

meaning: used to ask in a doubtful or incredulous tone
examples: 그건 내가 모르게? (As if I wouldn't know that?)
나 없이 갔을 게 뭐야? (Why would they have gone without me?)

meaning: used to ask about something the speaker has heard or been told
examples: 너 결혼한다면서? (I heard you're getting married?)
오늘 학교 안 간다면서요? (I heard you're not going to school today?)

meaning: informal question directed at addressee (retrospective check)
examples: 벌써 다 먹었나? (Did you already eat everything?)
집에 갔나? (Did they go home?)

meaning: I think it might be; I should have (conjecture or mild regret)
examples: 그 사람이 올걸요. (I think that person will probably come.)
이게 더 좋을걸. (I think this might be better.)

conjugation_rule: 아야지2 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여야지2 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해야지2); 어야지2 after all other stems
meaning: I really should; I must (self-directed resolution)
examples: 이제 더 열심히 해야지. (Now I really should work harder.)
내일은 일찍 일어나야지. (I really must wake up early tomorrow.)

meaning: used to add another question after the first one
examples: 무슨 일 있어요? 그리고요? (Is something going on? And what else?)
언제 왔어요? 혼자 왔고요? (When did you come? And alone?)

meaning: disbelief exclamation with reaction clause; how could (it be)
examples: 그게 사실이라니 믿을 수가 없어요! (I can't believe that's true!)
그 사람이 그런 말을 하다니 놀랍네요! (I can't believe that person said that!)

meaning: merely; nothing but; all one does is (limitation / sole action)
examples: 시키는 대로 할 따름이에요. (All I do is what I'm told.)
기다릴 따름이에요. (All I can do is wait.)

meaning: insist on doing (persistent/stubborn action)
examples: 아이가 자꾸 밖에 나가고 들어요. (The child keeps insisting on going outside.)
그 사람이 혼자 하겠다고 들어요. (That person keeps insisting on doing it alone.)

meaning: upon doing; after doing, one finds (retrospective discovery)
examples: 직접 해 보고 나니 쉽지 않네요. (After doing it myself, I found it's not easy.)
먹고 보니 맛있었어요. (After eating it, it turned out to be delicious.)

meaning: because; the reason being (colloquial reason)
examples: 바쁘고 해서 못 갔어요. (I couldn't go, partly because I was busy.)
비도 오고 해서 집에 있었어요. (I stayed home partly because it was also raining.)

meaning: while one is at it; on the occasion of (opportunistic action)
examples: 마트에 가는 김에 우유도 사왔어요. (While going to the market, I also bought milk.)
청소하는 김에 빨래도 했어요. (While cleaning, I also did the laundry.)

meaning: as soon as; following; as directed (immediacy / conformity)
examples: 오는 대로 바로 연락해요. (Contact me as soon as you arrive.)
시키는 대로 하면 돼요. (Just do as you're told.)

meaning: during; while; in the interval between (time gap)
examples: 제가 자리를 비운 사이에 전화가 왔어요. (A call came while I was away.)
밥 먹는 사이에 비가 왔어요. (It rained while I was eating.)

meaning: as if; as though (appearance / conjecture)
examples: 그 사람이 화난 듯 말했어요. (That person spoke as if angry.)
모르는 듯 행동했어요. (They acted as if they didn't know.)

meaning: that; whether; how to (knowing or not knowing something)
examples: 수영하는 줄 알았어요. (I thought they were swimming.)
거기 가는 줄 몰랐어요. (I didn't know they were going there.)

meaning: because of; owing to (negative cause / blame)
examples: 늦잠을 잔 탓에 지각했어요. (I was late because I overslept.)
비가 오는 탓에 소풍을 못 갔어요. (We couldn't go on the picnic because of the rain.)

meaning: listing alternatives; either ... or
examples: 온다거나 간다거나 연락은 해줘요. (Whether you are coming or going, please let me know.)
먹는다거나 안 먹는다거나 말은 해야지요. (Whether you will eat or not, you should say something.)

meaning: I think perhaps; wonder if (tentative conjecture)
examples: 이게 맞나 싶어요. (I wonder if this is right.)
그 사람이 오나 싶었는데 안 왔어요. (I wondered if they would come, but they didn't.)

meaning: because of; due to (unexpected negative cause)
examples: 갑자기 비가 오는 바람에 옷이 다 젖었어요. (My clothes got all wet because it suddenly rained.)
버스가 갑자기 서는 바람에 넘어졌어요. (I fell because the bus suddenly stopped.)

meaning: as long as; so long as (limiting condition)
examples: 네가 건강한 한 걱정 없어요. (As long as you are healthy, there's nothing to worry about.)
열심히 하는 한 실패하지 않아요. (As long as you work hard, you won't fail.)

related: 로 인하여, 으로, 인해, 로 인해
conjugation_rule: 하여 form contracts to 해 in colloquial speech
meaning: due to; because of (formal reason / cause)
examples: 폭설로 인하여 항공편이 취소됐어요. (The flight was cancelled due to heavy snow.)
전쟁으로 인하여 많은 사람이 고통받았어요. (Many people suffered due to war.)

meaning: if only it were like (unfulfilled wish)
examples: 젊을 때만 같아도 좋겠어요. (If only I were as young as before.)
예전만 같아도 더 잘 할 수 있었을 거예요. (If only it were like before, I could have done better.)

conjugation_rule: 아대다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여대다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해대다); 어대다 after all other stems
meaning: keep doing excessively; do repeatedly (repetitive / excessive action)
examples: 아이가 계속 울어 대요. (The child keeps crying.)
그 사람이 큰 소리로 떠들어 대요. (That person keeps talking loudly.)

conjugation_rule: 아서인지 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여서인지 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해서인지); 어서인지 after all other stems
meaning: perhaps because; might be because of
examples: 날씨가 추워서인지 사람이 별로 없어요. (Perhaps because it's cold, there aren't many people.)
피곤해서인지 집중이 안 돼요. (Perhaps because I'm tired, I can't concentrate.)

meaning: according to; depending on; in accordance with
examples: 상황에 따라 다르게 행동해요. (I act differently depending on the situation.)
날씨에 따라 옷을 입어요. (I dress according to the weather.)

meaning: compared to; in comparison with
examples: 작년에 비하여 많이 성장했어요. (Compared to last year, I have grown a lot.)
기대에 비하여 결과가 좋지 않아요. (Compared to expectations, the results aren't good.)

meaning: by; due to (passive agent / formal cause)
examples: 그 작품은 유명한 작가에 의하여 쓰였어요. (That work was written by a famous author.)
회의는 의장에 의하여 진행됐어요. (The meeting was conducted by the chairperson.)

conjugation_rule: 아버리다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여버리다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해버리다); 어버리다 after all other stems
meaning: go ahead and do; get it over with (completion — relief or regret)
examples: 음식을 다 먹어 버렸어요. (I finished all the food.)
지갑을 잃어버렸어요. (I lost my wallet.)

meaning: looks like; seems (conjecture from appearance or hearsay)
examples: 비가 올 모양이에요. (It looks like it will rain.)
그 사람이 화난 모양이에요. (It looks like that person is angry.)

meaning: almost; narrowly; was on the verge of (near-miss)
examples: 넘어질 뻔했어요. (I almost fell.)
늦을 뻔했어요. (I almost was late.)

meaning: indirect quotation (hearsay); reportedly; they say
examples: 오늘 일찍 온대요. (They say they're coming early today.)
그 사람이 간대요. (They say that person is leaving.)

meaning: because of the commotion of; due to the chaos of (chaotic cause)
examples: 아이들이 뛰는 통에 잠을 못 잤어요. (I couldn't sleep because of the children running around.)
공사 소음이 나는 통에 집중을 못 했어요. (I couldn't concentrate because of construction noise.)

meaning: or; such as (selection from examples)
examples: 사과라든가 배라든가 과일을 가져오세요. (Bring some fruit, like apples or pears.)
커피라든가 차라든가 마실 것을 준비해요. (Prepare something to drink, like coffee or tea.)

meaning: along; following (direction / accompanying particle — formal)
examples: 강을 따라 걸었어요. (I walked along the river.)
선생님을 따라 읽으세요. (Read along with the teacher.)

meaning: even; not even (extreme addition, usually negative)
examples: 이름조차 몰라요. (I don't even know the name.)
인사조차 안 해요. (They don't even say hello.)

meaning: used to show contrast, often in a formal or literary tone
examples: 갔지 오지는 않았어요. (They went but didn't come back.)
먹지 않고 갔어요. (They left without eating.)

meaning: not only ... but also (additive emphasis)
examples: 착할뿐더러 능력도 있어요. (Not only is he kind, but he's also capable.)
돈이 많을뿐더러 마음도 좋아요. (Not only does he have money, but he's also kind-hearted.)

meaning: after doing; and then (sequential with contrast or emphasis)
examples: 밥을 먹고는 바로 잠이 들었어요. (After eating, I fell asleep right away.)
숙제를 끝내고는 바로 나갔어요. (After finishing homework, I went out right away.)

meaning: because; that's why (colloquial reason from observation)
examples: 비가 오길래 우산을 가져왔어요. (I brought an umbrella because it was raining.)
그 사람이 오길래 인사했어요. (I greeted that person because they came.)

meaning: if one keeps doing; if this continues (warning of bad outcome)
examples: 그렇게 놀다가는 시험에 떨어져요. (If you keep playing like that, you'll fail the exam.)
이렇게 먹다가는 살이 찌겠어요. (If you keep eating like this, you'll gain weight.)

meaning: even if (strong concession — literary / formal)
examples: 아무리 참을지라도 한계가 있어요. (Even if I endure it, there is a limit.)
비가 올지라도 경기는 계속돼요. (Even if it rains, the game continues.)

meaning: used when comparing two options, often with the sense that one is better than the other
examples: 집에 있느니 나가는 게 나아요. (Going out is better than staying home.)
혼자 하느니 같이 하는 게 나아요. (Doing it together is better than doing it alone.)

meaning: used to express surprise or a strong immediate reaction
examples: 오늘 날씨 정말 좋네! (The weather today is really nice!)
그 사람이 벌써 왔네! (That person is already here!)

meaning: declarative statement (formal; newspaper style)
examples: 경찰이 범인을 잡았다. (The police caught the criminal.)
대통령이 순방을 마쳤다. (The president completed an overseas tour.)

meaning: standalone exclamation of disbelief or surprise (sentence-final)
examples: 그게 사실이라니! (The fact that it's true!)
그런 말을 하다니! (To think that person said such a thing!)

meaning: suspicion; doubt (questioning someone's intention)
examples: 지금 나가려고요? (Are you trying to go out now?)
혼자 하려고요? (Are you trying to do it alone?)

meaning: do (imperative — informal, used to address juniors)
examples: 빨리 가거라. (Go quickly.)
조용히 하거라. (Be quiet.)

conjugation_rule: -고말고 is the plain/informal form; -고말고요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: of course; absolutely (emphatic agreement)
examples: 당연하고말고요. (Of course!)
맞고말고. (Absolutely right.)

meaning: used to ask or wonder in a formal or literary style
examples: 지금 뭐 하는가? (What are you doing right now?)
거기 가는가? (Are you going there?)

meaning: you know; it seems (soft assertion / mild protest)
examples: 지금 집에 가는걸요. (I'm actually going home right now.)
배가 부른걸요. (I'm actually full.)

conjugation_rule: -데 is the plain/informal form; -데요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: it seems; you know (mild statement or exclamation of surprise)
examples: 그 음식이 맛있데요. (They say that food is delicious.)
그 영화 재미있데요. (They say that movie is fun.)

conjugation_rule: -더라고 is the plain/informal form; -더라고요 adds -요 for polite speech
meaning: it turned out that; I found that (retrospective report to listener)
examples: 그 음식이 맛있더라고요. (I found that food was delicious.)
생각보다 어렵더라고요. (It turned out harder than I thought.)

meaning: question about the content of a command/directive
examples: 지금 온다니요? (They're saying they're coming now?)
혼자 한다니 믿기 어려워요. (It's hard to believe they're doing it alone.)

meaning: was; used to be (past-attributive — discontinued past state)
examples: 예전에 살았던 동네가 많이 변했어요. (The neighborhood I used to live in has changed a lot.)
오래전에 다니던 학교에 다시 가 봤어요. (I visited the school I used to attend a long time ago.)

meaning: about; concerning; regarding (formal written)
examples: 환경 문제에 관하여 토론했어요. (We debated about environmental issues.)
그 사건에 관하여 보고서를 썼어요. (I wrote a report about that incident.)

meaning: because; as (literary reason linked to the action itself)
examples: 그 사람이 일찍 오자기에 서둘렀어요. (I hurried because that person said to come early.)
같이 먹자기에 식당에 갔어요. (I went to the restaurant because they suggested eating together.)

meaning: naturally; inevitably; is bound to (inherent truth)
examples: 사람은 실수를 하게 마련이에요. (People are bound to make mistakes.)
좋은 일이 있으면 나쁜 일도 있게 마련이에요. (If there are good things, there are bound to be bad things too.)

meaning: looks like it will; is bound to (unavoidable outcome by appearance)
examples: 이제 큰일이 생기게 생겼어요. (It looks like a big problem is going to happen.)
이대로 가다가는 실패하게 생겼어요. (The way things are going, we're bound to fail.)

meaning: it depends on; it's up to (outcome depends on how one does it)
examples: 결과는 네가 하기 나름이야. (The result depends on how you do it.)
성공은 노력하기 나름이에요. (Success depends on how much effort you put in.)

meaning: as soon as; no sooner than (immediately after)
examples: 집에 들어오기가 바쁘게 밥을 먹었어요. (As soon as I came home, I ate.)
학교에서 돌아오기가 바쁘게 잠이 들었어요. (No sooner had I returned from school than I fell asleep.)

meaning: tend to; easily (tendency or likelihood)
examples: 이 길은 미끄러지기가 쉬워요. (This road tends to be slippery.)
이 문제는 틀리기가 쉬워요. (This problem is easy to get wrong.)

meaning: just do; do nothing but; only do
examples: 그 아이는 울기만 해요. (That child just cries.)
공부하기만 했어요. (I did nothing but study.)

meaning: depending on how; according to how
examples: 노력하기에 따라 결과가 달라요. (The results vary depending on how you put in effort.)
먹기에 따라 건강이 달라져요. (Your health changes depending on how you eat.)

meaning: before; prior to (formal / written)
examples: 시작하기에 앞서 간단히 소개하겠습니다. (Before we begin, I will briefly introduce myself.)
회의를 시작하기에 앞서 출석을 확인하겠습니다. (Before starting the meeting, let's check attendance.)

meaning: as a result of (excessive state leading to outcome)
examples: 너무 웃은 나머지 눈물이 났어요. (I laughed so much that tears came out.)
너무 기쁜 나머지 눈물이 났어요. (I was so happy that tears came out.)

meaning: in addition to; on top of (additive)
examples: 날씨가 춥는 데다가 바람까지 불어요. (In addition to it being cold, the wind is blowing too.)
음식이 맛있는 데다가 가격도 싸요. (In addition to the food being delicious, the price is also low.)

meaning: at the same time as; simultaneously
examples: 요리하는 동시에 전화로 이야기해요. (I talk on the phone at the same time as I cook.)
공부하는 동시에 음악을 들어요. (I listen to music at the same time as I study.)

meaning: seems like; appears to (conjecture)
examples: 열심히 공부하는 듯해요. (It seems like they're studying hard.)
비가 올 듯해요. (It seems like it will rain.)

meaning: naturally; by nature; is the way things are (inevitable truth)
examples: 시간이 지나면 해결되는 법이에요. (With time, things naturally get resolved.)
노력하면 잘되는 법이에요. (If you try hard, things naturally work out.)

meaning: as long as; given that (premise-based obligation)
examples: 약속한 이상 지켜야 해요. (As long as you've made a promise, you must keep it.)
살아있는 이상 희망이 있어요. (As long as you're alive, there is hope.)

meaning: remaining in the state of (unchanged state)
examples: 신발을 신은 채로 들어오지 마세요. (Please don't come in with your shoes on.)
눈을 감은 채로 이야기했어요. (I talked with my eyes closed.)

meaning: pretend to; act as if (feigning)
examples: 그 사람은 모르는 척해요. (That person pretends not to know.)
자는 척하고 있어요. (They are pretending to sleep.)

meaning: in the midst of; while (ongoing context)
examples: 어려운 상황인 가운데 최선을 다했어요. (In the midst of difficult circumstances, I did my best.)
모든 사람이 보는 가운데 발표했어요. (I presented in front of everyone.)

related: 은 말할 것도 없고, <유의> 는 고사하고, 은 고사하고
meaning: needless to say; not to mention (obvious addition)
examples: 건강은 말할 것도 없고 행복도 중요해요. (Needless to say health, happiness is also important.)
일은 말할 것도 없고 휴식도 필요해요. (Needless to say work, rest is also necessary.)

meaning: worth doing; can reasonably do (worthiness / ability)
examples: 이 영화는 볼 만해요. (This movie is worth watching.)
그 식당은 가 볼 만해요. (That restaurant is worth visiting.)

meaning: likely to; seems probable (reasoned conjecture)
examples: 비가 올 법해요. (It seems probable that it will rain.)
그 사람이 올 법한데요. (It seems likely that person will come.)

meaning: intend to; will certainly (strong intention / prediction)
examples: 이번에는 꼭 합격할 테다. (This time, I will certainly pass.)
내가 직접 할 테다. (I intend to do it myself.)

meaning: if you are going to; since you insist (conditional challenge)
examples: 갈 테면 혼자 가세요. (If you're going to go, go alone.)
할 테면 해 보세요. (If you're going to do it, go ahead.)

meaning: although I think/expect it will (concessive supposition)
examples: 어렵겠을 테지만 노력해 봐요. (Although it may be difficult, let's try.)
힘들 테지만 포기하지 마세요. (Although it'll be hard, don't give up.)

meaning: seems like (someone) intends to; I wonder if (intention conjecture)
examples: 그 사람이 떠나려나 봐요. (It looks like that person is about to leave.)
비가 오려나 봐요. (It seems like it might rain.)

meaning: with; using; about (means or topic — colloquial)
examples: 그 문제를 가지고 고민했어요. (I thought hard about that problem.)
이걸 가지고 뭘 할 수 있어요? (What can be done with this?)

meaning: unless; if not for (exception condition)
examples: 비가 오면 몰라도 눈이 오지는 않아요. (It might rain, but it won't snow.)
그 사람이면 몰라도 나는 못 해요. (If it were that person maybe, but I can't do it.)

meaning: despite; in spite of; notwithstanding
examples: 비에도 불구하고 경기를 계속했어요. (Despite the rain, the game continued.)
어려움에도 불구하고 포기하지 않았어요. (Despite the difficulties, I did not give up.)

meaning: even though; despite (contrast against expectation)
examples: 공부를 열심히 하는데도 성적이 안 올라요. (Even though I study hard, my grades don't improve.)
잠을 잤는데도 피곤해요. (Even though I slept, I'm still tired.)

conjugation_rule: 아내다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여내다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해내다); 어내다 after all other stems
meaning: manage to do; accomplish (achieving against difficulty)
examples: 어렵게 문제를 풀어 냈어요. (I managed to solve the difficult problem.)
힘들게 일을 해 냈어요. (I managed to accomplish the difficult work.)

meaning: hearsay reason; because someone said (reported reason for action)
examples: 온다기에 기다렸어요. (I waited because they said they were coming.)
한다기에 믿었어요. (I trusted them because they said they would do it.)

meaning: meaning to do but (unintended divergence from plan)
examples: 칭찬한다는 것이 그만 나쁘게 말했어요. (Meaning to praise, I inadvertently said something bad.)
도와준다는 것이 오히려 방해가 됐어요. (Meaning to help, I instead got in the way.)

meaning: despite; regardless of (emphatic concession)
examples: 늦었는데도 불구하고 기다려 줬어요. (Despite being late, they waited for me.)
아픈데도 불구하고 출근했어요. (Despite being sick, I went to work.)

meaning: used to show contrast or opposition, often in a literary or old-fashioned tone
examples: 반대하다마는 어쩔 수 없어요. (I oppose it, but there's nothing I can do.)
가기 싫다마는 가야 해요. (I don't want to go, but I have to.)

meaning: used to say that something is quite considerable or that someone is fairly capable
examples: 그 사람 돈 깨나 있나 봐요. (That person seems to have quite a bit of money.)
한국어 공부 깨나 했겠네요. (You must have studied Korean quite a lot.)

meaning: used to dismiss something as insignificant or nothing special
examples: 이것이라고 별거 없어요. (This is really nothing special.)
그게 집이라고 할 수 있겠어요? (Can that even be called a house?)

meaning: colloquial topic particle used to bring up something with emphasis
examples: 이 일을랑 내가 할게요. (I'll take care of this.)
돈을랑 걱정하지 마세요. (Don't worry about the money.)

meaning: used to emphasize the topic, especially in the sense of when it comes to
examples: 고양이라면 알레르기가 있어요. (When it comes to cats, I'm allergic.)
한국 음식이라면 뭐든 좋아요. (When it comes to Korean food, I like anything.)

meaning: if; in case (conditional — dialect / archaic)
examples: 비가 오거들랑 전화해요. (If it rains, call me.)
시간이 나거들랑 놀러 와요. (If you have free time, come visit.)

meaning: as one keeps doing; if one continues to (progressive condition)
examples: 연습하노라면 언젠가 잘하게 돼요. (If you keep practicing, someday you'll get good at it.)
사노라면 좋은 일도 생기는 법이에요. (As you keep on living, good things are bound to happen.)

meaning: in my opinion; I believe (speaker's opinion — literary)
examples: 생각건대 그 방법이 최선이에요. (In my opinion, that method is best.)
바라건대 모두 건강하길 바랍니다. (I hope everyone will be healthy.)

meaning: but; however (concession with disappointment — literary)
examples: 열심히 하건만 결과가 안 나와요. (I work hard, but the results don't come.)
노력은 했건만 실패했어요. (I made effort, but I failed.)

meaning: even so; even granting that (concession — literary / archaic)
examples: 이미 결정했기로서니 바꿀 수 없어요. (Even so, since it's already decided, we can't change it.)
늦었기로서니 지금이라도 시작해요. (Even so that it's late, let's start now at least.)

meaning: inasmuch as; since (proportional reason — literary)
examples: 노력하느니만큼 결과를 기대할 수 있어요. (Inasmuch as you put in effort, you can expect results.)
공부하느니만큼 실력이 쌓여요. (Inasmuch as you study, skills accumulate.)

meaning: but; however (contrast — formal / written)
examples: 성실하되 유연하게 일하세요. (Work diligently but flexibly.)
규칙은 지키되 상황을 봐 가며 해요. (Follow the rules, but do so with an eye on the situation.)

meaning: used to add emphasis, often when challenging or pushing back against someone
examples: 잘 보이디 어디 봐라. (Let's see how well you really do.)
네가 그렇게 잘하디 한번 해 봐라. (If you're really that good, go ahead and try it.)

meaning: only if it is; must be (exclusive necessary condition)
examples: 진심이라야 마음이 통해요. (Only if it's sincere will hearts connect.)
노력이라야 성공할 수 있어요. (Only through effort can one succeed.)

meaning: I would but; I could but (unfulfilled wish — literary)
examples: 갈 수 있으련마는 시간이 없어요. (I could go, but I have no time.)
말하고 싶으련마는 참아요. (I want to say it, but I'll hold back.)

meaning: intention (expressing what one intends to do)
examples: 먹자니 배가 부르고 안 먹자니 아깝다. (If I eat, I'm full; if I don't eat, it's a waste.)
가자니 멀고 안 가자니 보고 싶다. (Going is far, but not going means I'll miss them.)

meaning: intention (expressing a directive or intention to do something)
examples: 빨리 온다고! (Come quickly, I said!)
조용히 한다고 했잖아요! (I told you to be quiet!)

meaning: if one intends to; in order to; if one wants to
examples: 빨리 가자면 택시를 타야 해요. (If we want to go quickly, we should take a taxi.)
많이 먹자면 돈이 많아야 해요. (To eat a lot, you need a lot of money.)

meaning: even if; even though (literary concession implying futility)
examples: 돈이 있은들 행복하지 않아요. (Even if there is money, I'm not happy.)
말한들 소용없어요. (Even if I say it, it's no use.)

meaning: even though; even if (strong concession — literary)
examples: 힘들망정 포기는 하지 않겠어요. (Even though it's difficult, I won't give up.)
가난할망정 행복하게 살아요. (Even though I'm poor, I live happily.)

meaning: whenever; each time (conditional circumstance)
examples: 생각을 정리할라치면 전화가 와요. (Whenever I try to organize my thoughts, a call comes.)
자리에 앉을라치면 누가 불러요. (Whenever I try to sit down, someone calls me.)

meaning: used to express surprise or admiration in an old-fashioned tone
examples: 오늘도 열심히 일하는구려. (You are working hard again today.)
그 친구가 이미 가는구려. (That friend is already leaving.)

meaning: well, it is! (exclamation of realization — informal / elderly speech)
examples: 어, 비가 오는구먼. (Oh, it's raining.)
그 사람이 또 늦는구먼. (That person is late again.)

meaning: I see; is that so (soft agreement / realization — archaic)
examples: 그러시그려, 그렇게 하지요. (I see, let's do it that way.)
참 좋은 생각이그려. (What a good idea.)

meaning: will; must be (strong conjecture or literary will)
examples: 내년에는 꼭 합격하리라. (I will certainly pass next year.)
언젠가는 돌아오리라. (I will surely return someday.)

meaning: (formal polite ending — 하오체, old-style formal register)
examples: 잠시 기다리시오. (Please wait a moment.)
이리로 오시오. (Please come this way.)

meaning: used to scold or rebuke someone
examples: 왜 이제 왔으니? (Why did you come only now?)
그때 왜 말 안 했으니? (Why didn't you say something then?)

meaning: used to mention a reason or situation before giving a response
examples: 바쁘거들랑 나중에 해요. (If you're busy, do it later.)
필요하거들랑 가져가요. (If you need it, take it.)

meaning: used to give a command, often in an authoritative or old-fashioned tone
examples: 어서 오게. (Come in quickly.)
빨리 먹게. (Eat quickly.)

meaning: used to remind someone of something or draw their attention to it
examples: 이제 가게. 기다리고 있을게. (Go now. I'll be waiting.)
잊지 말게. 약속한 거야. (Don't forget. You promised.)

meaning: used to give a command in an old-fashioned style
examples: 자리에 앉으구려. (Please take a seat.)
빨리 오구려. (Please come quickly.)

meaning: exclamatory declaration; expressing surprise or realization
examples: 벌써 10시가 됐네! (Oh, it is already 10 o'clock!)
여기서 만나네! (Oh, we are meeting here!)

meaning: oh really! (exclamation — informal / elderly speech)
examples: 오늘도 열심히 일하는구만. (Working hard again today, I see.)
그 사람이 또 오는구만. (That person is coming again, I see.)

meaning: raising a question or issue; rhetorical question
examples: 이게 과연 맞는가 생각해 봤어요. (I thought about whether this was truly correct.)
지금 무엇을 하는가 물어봤어요. (I asked what they were doing now.)

meaning: self-directed wonder or rhetorical question (literary)
examples: 어디서 그가 살고 있나 궁금해요. (I wonder where he is living.)
과연 이게 옳은 길이나 생각해 봤어요. (I wondered to myself whether this was truly the right path.)

meaning: used to ask or wonder in an old-fashioned tone
examples: 그때 거기서 무슨 일이 있었던? (What happened there at that time?)
예전에 그 사람이 왔던? (Did that person come before?)

meaning: question about past experience (recalling something one experienced)
examples: 어제 어디 갔던가? (Where did you go yesterday?)
그 영화 재미있었던가? (Was that movie interesting?)

meaning: question based on inference or supposition (recalling something one assumed)
examples: 작년에 거기 가셨던가요? (Did you go there last year?)
그분이 누구셨던가요? (Who was that person?)

meaning: declarative statement (literary; poetic register)
examples: 해가 밝아 오는 아침이라. (It is a morning with the bright sun rising.)
꽃이 피는 봄이라. (It is a spring when flowers bloom.)

meaning: will it? (rhetorical question — classical / literary)
examples: 그게 사실이리오? (Could that be true?)
나 없이 어찌 살리오? (How could you live without me?)

meaning: it is; please do (formal polite ending — 하오체)
examples: 거기 있으소. (Please stay there.)
잠시 기다리소. (Please wait a moment.)

meaning: often does; habitually (frequent or characteristic negative behavior)
examples: 그 사람은 지각하기 일쑤예요. (That person habitually comes late.)
그 아이는 울기 일쑤예요. (That child cries all the time.)

meaning: extremely; incomparably (superlative emphasis, often negative)
examples: 그 사람은 귀찮기 짝이 없어요. (That person is incomparably annoying.)
이 상황은 어이없기 짝이 없어요. (This situation is unbelievably absurd.)

meaning: at the point when; given the situation (critical juncture)
examples: 이미 결혼한 마당에 이제 어쩔 수가 없어요. (Given that they're already married, there's nothing to be done now.)
이렇게 된 마당에 최선을 다해야 해요. (Given the situation, we have to do our best.)

meaning: even if it means; no matter what (willingness to accept extreme)
examples: 죽는 한이 있어도 포기 못 해요. (Even if it means death, I can't give up.)
힘든 한이 있어도 해 내겠어요. (Even if it means hardship, I will accomplish it.)

meaning: if one is going to do anyway; might as well (choosing lesser evil)
examples: 이렇게 살 바에 차라리 포기하는 게 나아요. (If I'm going to live like this, it would be better to give up.)
거짓말을 할 바에 차라리 말을 안 하는 게 낫죠. (If I'm going to lie, it's better to say nothing.)

meaning: even if one tries to (attempt against an obstacle)
examples: 가려도 갈 수가 없어요. (Even if I try to go, I can't.)
자려도 잠이 안 와요. (Even if I try to sleep, I can't.)

meaning: that (someone) will; predicted to (quoted conjecture)
examples: 꼭 합격하리라는 믿음이 있어요. (I have the belief that I will certainly pass.)
그가 돌아오리라는 희망을 버리지 않았어요. (I did not give up the hope that he would return.)

related: 을 막론하고, 를 불문하고, 을 불문하고
meaning: regardless of; whether or not (ignoring distinction)
examples: 남녀를 막론하고 모두 참여했어요. (Everyone participated regardless of gender.)
나이를 막론하고 누구나 올 수 있어요. (Anyone can come regardless of age.)

conjugation_rule: 아치우다 when the verb/adjective stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ; 여치우다 after 하다 stems (contracted to 해치우다); 어치우다 after all other stems
meaning: get it over with; do away with decisively (decisive completion)
examples: 남은 음식을 먹어 치웠어요. (I finished off the remaining food.)
오래된 문제를 해결해 치웠어요. (I got the old problem over with.)

meaning: the claim or plan that (quoted statement / intention)
examples: 그가 온다는 소식을 들었어요. (I heard the news that he is coming.)
공부를 열심히 한다는 말은 믿기 어려워요. (It's hard to believe the claim that they study hard.)

meaning: not even one; not even as (extreme limiting statement)
examples: 친구라고는 한 명도 없어요. (There isn't even a single friend.)
쓸 만한 것이라고는 없어요. (There's nothing worth using.)

meaning: question confirming a command; asking about the content of an order or directive
examples: 온다던가 뭐라던가 했어요. (They said something like they are coming or something.)
내일 간다던가 했는데 잘 모르겠어요. (They said something like going tomorrow, but I am not sure.)

meaning: used to challenge or object to an instruction or demand
examples: 그렇게 하래서야 어떻게 따르겠어요? (How can I follow if you tell me to do it like that?)
매일 오래서야 힘들지 않겠어요? (Wouldn't it be hard if they tell you to come every day?)

meaning: that (someone) will; reported conjecture (quoted conjecture)
examples: 꼭 성공하리라고 믿어요. (I believe I will certainly succeed.)
그가 돌아오리라고 생각해요. (I think he will return.)

meaning: used when referring to or quoting a suggestion such as "let's ..."
examples: 먹자면 배가 고프다는 뜻이에요. (Saying "Let's eat" means the speaker is hungry.)
자자면 피곤하거나 늦은 거죠. (Saying "Let's sleep" means the speaker is tired or that it is late.)
